title: Cellular Automata: EML Depth of Emergent Computation

elementary_ca_rule110.key_insight: Rule 110: EML-∞ — universal computation — Turing complete

rules.reason: Turing complete: any TM computation ⟹ any EML depth reachable

glider.note: Glider = EML-0 periodic; Glider gun = EML-∞ (generates universal computation)

halting_problem.eml_connection: Same EML-∞ stratum as Gödel sentence, CH, RH (if undecidable)

game_of_life.key_insight: GoL rules = EML-0; GoL dynamics = EML-∞ (Turing complete, halting undecidable)

complexity_measures.key_insight: CA entropy = EML-2; Lyapunov (chaotic) = EML-3; Kolmogorov complexity = EML-∞

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: CA rule tables, cell states, glider periods, Chern numbers
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Spatial entropy, compression ratio, fractal dimension (log ratio)
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Class III chaotic CA (random-appearing), Lyapunov exponents
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Class IV (universal computation), Game of Life, extinction question (halting)

key_theorem: The EML Cellular Automata Depth Theorem: A CA rule is a finite lookup table — EML-0. The behavior it generates can be EML-0 (Class I/II), EML-3 (Class III), or EML-∞ (Class IV). This is the EML version of Wolfram's universality: EML-0 local rules can generate EML-∞ global behavior. The key EML-∞ threshold: Turing completeness ↔ halting problem ↔ EML-∞ undecidability. The same threshold as Gödel: from EML-0 axioms, reach EML-∞ statements.
rabbit_hole_log: Rule table = EML-0: 8-bit lookup, finite, no computation
Rule 30 = EML-3: chaotic output used in Mathematica PRNG
Rule 110 = EML-∞: Turing complete (Cook 2004) — EML-0 rules → EML-∞ computation
Sierpinski triangle dim = log3/log2 = EML-2 (log ratio of integers)
GoL extinction = halting problem = EML-∞: same stratum as Gödel/CH/RH
EML-0 rules → EML-∞ behavior: the deepest depth-generating pattern in EML theory

connections.S139_foundations_v2: Gödel (EML-∞) ↔ GoL halting (EML-∞): both from finite axioms/rules
connections.S152_chaos_control: Class III CA = chaotic attractor: both EML-3
connections.S150_grand_synthesis_9: EML-0 → EML-∞ depth generation confirms the Horizon Theorem
